steps: Copy Avatar_Sky_<version>.img to the root of the VTX's SD card; delete any older .img first.
Insert the SD into the VTX.
Power on the VTX. The upgrade runs automatically and is signalled by the status LEDs.
Keep power connected until the normal boot LED pattern returns.
Confirm the new version in the goggles OSD after reboot.
Avatar GT / GT2 units require firmware >= 39.44.2; flashing below that is a brick risk and the app warns before proceeding.
Copy AvatarMini_Sky_<version>.img to the Mini VTX SD card root; remove any older .img.
Insert the SD into the VTX.
Power on. The Mini VTX flashes automatically; the status LED blinks during the process.
Wait until the status LED returns to its normal steady pattern before disconnecting power.
Check the new version in the OSD after reboot.
Copy AvatarMoonlight_Sky_<version>.img to the Moonlight SD card root.
For a DVR-board-only upgrade, also drop an empty independ_upgrade.txt in the SD root — the app can do this automatically via the checkbox in the staging wizard.
Connect the JST jumper between the Moonlight camera and VTX modules.
Power on the Moonlight and wait for the LEDs to stabilise.
Press and hold the unit's button for about 8 seconds to start the update.
Keep power connected until the LEDs return to their normal boot pattern.
Verify the new version in the goggles OSD after reboot.
Copy Avatar_Gnd_<version>.img to the goggles SD card root; delete any older .img first.
Insert the SD card into the goggles.
Power on the goggles. An upgrade prompt appears on the display — confirm it.
Do not power off until the upgrade-success screen is shown.
Check the new version in the goggles menu after reboot.
Copy AvatarX_Gnd_<version>.img to the Goggles X SD card root. Do not rename the file. Delete any older firmware images first.
Insert the SD card into the Goggles X.
Power on the goggles. An on-screen prompt asks you to confirm the upgrade.
Keep the goggles powered until the success screen appears.
Verify the new version in the goggles menu.
CRITICAL: Goggles X manufactured since January 2025 use new DDR memory. Flashing below 38.44.13 is a permanent brick. The app refuses to stage firmware below that floor.
Copy AvatarLite_Gnd_<version>.img to the Goggles L SD card root; delete older .img files first.
Insert the SD card into the goggles.
Power on and accept the on-screen upgrade prompt.
Keep the unit powered until the success screen is shown.
Verify the new version in the goggles menu after reboot.
Copy AvatarSE_Gnd_<version>.img to the VRX SD card root.
Insert the SD card into the VRX.
Power on. The LEDs indicate upgrade progress.
Wait for the normal LED pattern before disconnecting power.
Confirm the firmware version via the VRX status indication (or in the receiving application).
Copy AvatarMini_Gnd_<version>.img to the Recon HD SD card root.
Insert the SD card into the Recon HD receiver.
Power on. The upgrade runs automatically and is signalled by the LEDs.
Keep power connected until the LED pattern returns to normal.
Check the new firmware version after reboot.
Copy Avatar_Relay_<version>.img to the Relay SD card root.
Insert the SD card into the Relay module.
Power on the Relay. The LEDs indicate upgrade progress.
Wait for the normal LED pattern before disconnecting power.
Verify the new version via the Relay status output.
